message:
fix(hooks): allow rm -rf on temp directories

The git_safety_guard.py hook was blocking all rm -rf commands, including
legitimate cleanup of temp directories like /tmp, /var/tmp, and $TMPDIR.

This was overly restrictive since temp directories are designed for
ephemeral data that can be safely deleted.

Added safe patterns to allowlist:
- rm -rf /tmp/...
- rm -rf /var/tmp/...
- rm -rf $TMPDIR/...
- rm -rf "${TMPDIR}/..." (quoted variants)

Non-temp paths are still blocked, as are all other destructive patterns
(git checkout --, git reset --hard, etc.).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -86,6 +86,13 @@
     r"git\s+restore\s+--staged\s+",      # Unstaging (safe)
     r"git\s+clean\s+-n",                 # Dry run
     r"git\s+clean\s+--dry-run",          # Dry run
+    # Allow rm -rf on temp directories (these are designed for ephemeral data)
+    r"rm\s+-[a-z]*r[a-z]*f[a-z]*\s+/tmp/",        # /tmp/...
+    r"rm\s+-[a-z]*r[a-z]*f[a-z]*\s+/var/tmp/",    # /var/tmp/...
+    r"rm\s+-[a-z]*r[a-z]*f[a-z]*\s+\$TMPDIR/",    # $TMPDIR/...
+    r"rm\s+-[a-z]*r[a-z]*f[a-z]*\s+\$\{TMPDIR",   # ${TMPDIR}/... or ${TMPDIR:-...}
+    r'rm\s+-[a-z]*r[a-z]*f[a-z]*\s+"\$TMPDIR/',   # "$TMPDIR/..."
+    r'rm\s+-[a-z]*r[a-z]*f[a-z]*\s+"\$\{TMPDIR',  # "${TMPDIR}/..." or "${TMPDIR:-...}"
 ]
